name: Mill-limited (the classic)
expected_band: the mill binds -> the cut-off is raised above break-even by f+F.delta; high-grading pays
validation_anchor: mean cut-off > break-even; the cut-off declines over the life

engine.model: Lane's economic cut-off grade: a lognormal grade-tonnage curve + the three limiting + three balancing cut-offs (with the f + F*delta opportunity-cost term) + an exact year-by-year NPV life simulator + the iterative DECLINING high-grading trajectory. The same TS engine runs live in the browser and in the offline bake. The cut-off/NPV surrogate (torch->ONNX) runs live via onnxruntime-web; the EXACT optimizer is the baseline + the authority.

flags: mill_capacity >= mine_capacity: the mill can never bind (all mined ore fits)
mill_capacity >= mine_capacity: the mill can never bind (all mined ore fits)

honesty: The deposits + economics are SYNTHETIC (a porphyry-copper-like base case), stated openly; C-UNIFORM and C-BREAKEVEN are closed-form controls. The cut-off surrogate is measured against the EXACT Lane optimizer (NPV + cut-off error); the OOD autoencoder flags scenarios outside the training envelope. Reported whichever way the numbers land. No fabricated win.
